_comment: Curated plain-English 'what it does in the game' descriptions. Module-level entries label the architecture overview; function-level entries (keyed by 'module::short') override JSDoc/humanized labels for important hubs. Anything not listed falls back to its JSDoc comment, then to a humanized version of its name.

groups.core: Static game data + the immutable GameState container. Definitions only — the shape of the world, no behaviour.
groups.services: Business-logic singletons. Each owns one domain (jobs, items, pawns, research...) and is the only place that domain's rules live.
groups.systems: Cross-cutting engines that orchestrate services: the turn coordinator, the pawn AI state machine, combat resolution, and the stat modifier pipeline.
groups.stores: Svelte reactive stores. The bridge between the game engine and the UI; also owns save/load to localStorage.
groups.entities: Factory + helpers for spawning pawns (the characters the player manages).
groups.utils: Small stateless helpers shared across pawns and work logic.
groups.dev: Developer-only logging and scratch-world tooling. Not part of shipped gameplay.
groups.webgl: The custom WebGL renderer: tile grid, font atlas, shaders, textures. Draws the map; holds no game rules.
groups.world: World generation and map rendering glue.

modules.game/core/GameState: The immutable game-state container + GameStateManager. EVERY change to the world goes through its methods (addResource, updatePawn, consumeFromStockpiles...). Nothing mutates state directly.
modules.game/core/types: The master type vocabulary — Pawn, Item, Building, Resource, Tile, GameState. The shared language every layer speaks.
modules.game/core/rng: Seeded deterministic RNG. Same seed = same world & rolls, so runs are reproducible. The single most-called primitive in the codebase.
modules.game/core/time: Turn/tick time helpers — converts the turn counter into days, seasons, day/night.
modules.game/core/needs: Definitions of pawn needs (hunger, thirst, rest) and their decay thresholds.
modules.game/core/Race: Procedural race generation — rolls a species' stats, traits, and starting profile at new-game.
modules.game/core/Terrains: Terrain & subterrain definitions, movement cost, and what each tile yields.
modules.game/core/PawnEquipment: How equipped weapons/armour translate into a pawn's effective combat & carry profile.
modules.game/core/Events: Static random-event definitions and their trigger conditions.
modules.game/core/Wounds: Wound definitions and severity tables applied during combat.
modules.game/services/JobService: The work queue brain. Scans designations + buildings and generates concrete jobs (harvest, haul, construct, craft, refuel), then advances and completes them. Implements ADR-016 reserve-and-fetch crafting.
modules.game/services/PawnService: Owns pawn lifecycle and per-turn pawn updates outside the state machine — needs decay, mood, health, skill gain.
modules.game/services/PawnStatService: Computes a pawn's effective stats by running base stats through the modifier pipeline (traits, equipment, mood, wounds).
modules.game/services/EntityService: Master registry/factory for world entities and the items database loader. The biggest service — resolves items, recipes, and their derived properties.
modules.game/services/ItemService: Item definitions, lookup, stack rules, durability/quality, and what an item does when used.
modules.game/services/BuildingService: Building definitions, placement validity, construction cost, condition/decay, and what a finished building provides.
modules.game/services/ResearchService: The tech tree — what's unlocked, research progress per turn, and gating of recipes/buildings behind discoveries.
modules.game/services/RecipeService: Maps an output item to the recipe (inputs, station, work) needed to craft it.
modules.game/services/DesignationService: Player intent layer — the tiles/objects the player has marked for harvesting, building, or deconstruction. Feeds JobService.
modules.game/services/WorkService: Defines work types and resolves a pawn's speed/yield/quality for a given job via stats.jsonc (the single work model, ADR-015).
modules.game/services/ResourceObjectService: Tracks placed world resource objects (trees, rocks, ore) — their position, remaining yield, and depletion.
modules.game/services/ResourceGeneratorService: Seeds the map with resource objects at world-gen and on regrowth.
modules.game/services/EnvironmentService: Ambient world conditions — temperature, weather, season effects on pawns and work.
modules.game/services/LightingService: Light levels per tile (daylight, fire, lamps) and how darkness penalises work and vision.
modules.game/services/OccupancyService: Tracks which tiles are occupied so pawns and buildings don't overlap.
modules.game/services/PathfinderService: TypeScript interface in front of pathfinding (ADR-008). Callers depend on this, never on the WASM directly.
modules.game/services/WasmPathfinderService: Rust/WASM A* implementation behind the PathfinderService interface.
modules.game/systems/GameEngineImpl: The turn coordinator. Runs the fixed pipeline each turn — needs -> work -> completions -> exploration -> events — calling into services in order. The heartbeat of the simulation.
modules.game/systems/PawnStateMachine: The pawn AI. Decides what each pawn does moment-to-moment: pick a job, path to it, satisfy needs, fight, sleep. The largest single file and a known complexity hotspot.
modules.game/systems/Combat: Resolves fights — to-hit, damage, wounds, death — between pawns and creatures.
modules.game/systems/ModifierSystem: The stat/efficiency pipeline. Every computed stat passes through here and carries sources[] explaining where each bonus came from.
modules.game/systems/MovementSystem: Applies movement along a path, tile by tile, respecting movement cost.
modules.game/entities/Pawns: Pawn factory — rolls a new character's name, stats, traits, and starting state.
modules.game/utils/pawnUtils: Shared pawn helpers (position, inventory, capability checks) used by services and the state machine.
modules.game/utils/workUtils: Shared helpers for resolving work assignments and priorities.
modules.stores/gameState: The central Svelte store wrapping GameState. The UI reads from here; advancing a turn flows engine -> store -> components, and it auto-saves.
modules.stores/saveManager: Serialises/deserialises the whole game to localStorage; handles save versioning.
modules.stores/Log: The in-game activity log feed shown to the player.
modules.stores/eventStore: Holds the currently active event awaiting player choice.
modules.stores/combatFeedback: Transient combat popups/floating numbers for the UI.
modules.stores/worldEffects: Visual world effects state (weather overlays, etc.).
modules.stores/uiState: Misc UI state (selected pawn, open panel).
modules.game/dev/gameLogger: Verbose developer trace of simulation steps.
modules.game/dev/activityLogger: Records pawn activity for debugging behaviour.
modules.game/dev/devWorld: Builds a deterministic test world for development.
modules.game/world/WorldGenerator: Generates the map at new-game — terrain, elevation, biomes via noise.
modules.game/world/MapRenderer: Glue that hands world data to the WebGL renderer.

functions.game/systems/GameEngineImpl::GameEngineI: Advance the world by one turn — runs the whole needs -> work -> completions -> exploration -> events pipeline once.
functions.game/services/JobService::JobServiceImpl: Rebuild the job queue: turn player designations and building needs into concrete jobs pawns can pick up.
Push one job forward by a tick of work, completing it when the work is done.
functions.game/core/rng::SeededRng.random: Draw the next deterministic random number — the seed makes every run reproducible.
functions.game/systems/ModifierSystem::ModifierSys: Run a base value through all applicable modifiers and return the result plus the list of sources that changed it.
functions.game/core/GameState::consumeFromStockpil: Remove resources from storage to pay a cost, pulling from whichever stockpiles hold them.
functions.game/services/BuildingService::BuildingS: Look up a building definition by its stable id.
functions.game/services/ItemService::ItemServiceIm: Look up an item definition by its stable id.
functions.game/services/ResearchService::ResearchS: Look up a tech-tree node by its stable id.
